paper_title: Extracting Common Time Trends from Concurrent Time Series: Maximum Autocorrelation Factors with Application to Tree Ring Time Series Data

methods.description: This method involves simulating parallel time series data to evaluate the performance of Maximum Autocorrelation Factors (MAF) and Principal Component Analysis (PCA) in extracting common time trends.

steps.description: We do 100 simulations of p = 3 parallel time series of length n = 150, using the S+N model of Section 2.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate 100 parallel time series using S+N model

steps.description: The time series is a rescaled and interpolated version of the mean annual surface time series for the northern hemisphere for the years 1850-2007.
steps.searchable_summary: rescale and interpolate time series data

steps.description: Two simulations of the data are shown with their associated smoothed MAF and PCA time series.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze simulations with MAF and PCA
outputs: smoothed MAF and PCA time series

methods.description: This method applies Maximum Autocorrelation Factors (MAF) to tree ring data to extract common underlying temporal signals.

steps.description: We look at tree ring data from western United States obtained from Mann et al. [2008].
steps.searchable_summary: fetch tree ring data from Mann et al. [2008]

steps.description: The data has been pre-processed as described in Mann et al 's Supplemental section.
We selected 21 concurrent tree ring time series for the period 1850-1999.
steps.searchable_summary: select 21 concurrent tree ring time series

steps.description: The 3 first MAFs and PCs are shown where each time series is annotated by its sample autocorrelation.
Sampling uncertainty estimates of the MAF factors can also be obtained through the block bootstrap.
steps.searchable_summary: perform block bootstrap for MAF uncertainty estimation

steps.description: Hypothesis testing is performed using the MAF SNR estimate as the test statistic.
steps.searchable_summary: perform hypothesis testing using MAF SNR
